name: madhubani-tee-catalog
description: The 100-entry Indian-wildlife catalog for Madhubani-inspired t-shirt designs, organized across 25 Indian national parks (2 mammals + 2 birds per park). v3.0.0 expands v2 from 40 entries / 20 parks to 100 entries / 25 parks: bird representation grows from ~6 to ~50, four new parks added (nagarhole, great-himalayan, khangchendzonga, bhitarkanika). The parks block is removed from this file — canonical park metadata lives at brand/parks/_index.json. Each animal entry's `park` field is the join key.

broad shoulders and hips

all four legs clearly visible in side profile

all four legs visible as distinct outlines

slender legs proportional to body

proportional hands and feet
tail length appropriate to species
expressive face with intentional gravity
body plates simplified into folk panels
all four legs visible
characteristic tail visible
streamlined body silhouette
fluke and dorsal fin visible
water ground-mark of folk dots instead of land
heavy bulky body with low ground clearance
shorter sturdy legs proportional to thick torso

all four legs visible in side profile
blunt snout proportional to head mass
compact body with proportional short-to-medium legs
all four legs clearly visible in side profile

almond eye not round cartoon eye

four short laterally-splayed legs all visible
long muscular tail equal to or longer than body
scaled back ridge translated into rhythmic folk dorsal panels
elongated snout with closed-mouth default, teeth NOT shown unless action pose

indian.species_per_park_split: 2 mammals + 2 birds (with 3 documented exceptions: chilika lagoon, marine-kutch coastal, keoladeo bird-sanctuary — see brand/parks/_index.json _2_2_rule_breakers)

_deprecated_series._note: The v1.0.0 catalog split entries across three series — Indian / African / Bird — at seed ranges 8200-8999, 9000-9799, 9800-10599. v2.0.0 collapses everything into a single 'indian' series focused on the 20-national-park catalog. The african and bird series are preserved here only as historical record; new entries should NOT use these series tags.

bird.display_name: Birds (DEPRECATED as standalone series in v2.0.0 — birds are now embedded inside the indian series, e.g. sarus-crane, painted-stork, indian-skimmer)

_parks_block_removed_in_v3: Park metadata previously embedded here has been moved to brand/parks/_index.json (canonical source). Per-animal `park` field below is the join key. Audit (2026-05-22) confirmed no code reads the old parks block — only individual animals' `park` slug field. See `_parks_canonical_source` above.

animals.species_render_name_reason: N1-rev2 (2026-05-20, post FLUX.2 migration + L1+L2): FLUX.2 honors body_fill_color but still pulls the tiger toward photorealism (3D face, fur, dimensional muscle) when the subject says 'Royal Bengal Tiger'. Earlier N1 attempt used 'folk-art striped large cat' which dropped the species identity entirely and made it cartoonish. This phrasing keeps 'tiger' (preserves anatomy + stripes) but anchors it as a 'Madhubani folk-art icon' (anchors style). Combined with L1+L2's flat-silhouette directives, this might break the photoreal lock without losing tiger character. display_name stays canonical for manifests/QC/files; only the rendered subject is neutralized.
animals.signature_features: the body silhouette MUST carry 8-12 BOLD VERTICAL STRIPES — the iconic tiger stripe rhythm, preserved as flat folk-color bands running top-to-bottom across the back/flank/shoulder/haunch in indigo/leaf-green/vermillion/gold (NEVER black-on-orange photography, NEVER replaced by floral medallions). Without those distinct vertical stripe bands this is not a tiger. Plus: long curved tail with a continuation of the stripe-bands as tail rings, almond eye, whisker dots near the muzzle.
animals.signature_action: crouched ready to leap, low body, intense gaze forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated upright, forelimbs straight, tail wrapped around feet
animals.anatomy_must_include: body filled with saturated walnut-brown NOT natural orange-and-black tiger photography; THE STRIPE PATTERN MUST READ AS STRIPES — 8-12 distinct vertical bands of flat folk color (indigo, leaf-green, vermillion, gold) running across the back/flank/shoulder; NOT scattered floral medallions, NOT all-over patterning, NOT a generic lion silhouette; almond eye not round cartoon eye; long curved tail with banded folk pattern; the result must be unambiguously a TIGER (vertical stripe rhythm visible) and not a lion or generic large cat
animals._style_reference_disabled_reason: Phase-A 2026-05-20: the v3 tiger ref is sparse (orange + black stripes only; no interior Madhubani decoration). At strength 0.55-0.67 it OVERRODE the prompt's MANDATORY DECORATION ZONES, producing tigers with zero interior art. Prompt-only render is dramatically richer. Disabled until a denser tiger reference is curated or the variant tier ships a Bharni-dense LoRA.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: small circular tikka medallion in vermillion + saffron between the eyes
NECK: multi-color folk collar band (3-5 colors: indigo/saffron/vermillion/leaf-green)

BODY INTERIOR (most important): inside each of the 8-12 vertical stripe bands, paint flat folk-color panels with lotus medallions, leaf-vein details, and dot patterns — the stripe bands ARE the canvas for decoration, not empty black bars
SHOULDER + HIP: large ornamental rosette panels (vermillion floral with saffron center)
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + leaf-green folk bands at each of the four leg joints
TAIL: continuation of stripe bands as folk-color rings (NOT plain black bars)

anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 1 in side profile (almond eye, single eye visible)
anatomical_count_constraints.mouth: closed (no tongue visible; no fangs unless signature-action pose)

animals.conservation_note: National animal of India; ~3000 wild individuals remain. Jim Corbett National Park is India's oldest national park (1936) and the launchpad of Project Tiger.

animals.signature_features: curved trunk, large decorated ear, small tusk, almond eye
animals.signature_action: trumpeting trunk raised high, mouth open in call
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing relaxed, trunk curled, weight on three legs with one lifted
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: tikka medallion + decorated headband
EAR: large folk-pattern panel filling the visible ear (vine motifs, dot bands, vermillion accents)
NECK: multi-color folk collar
BODY: ornate decorated saddle blanket covering the back with lotus medallion at center, leaf-vein border, vermillion + saffron palette
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron + cream folk bands at each ankle
TUSKS: visible small tusks (proportional, not exaggerated)
TAIL: short with tufted tip

anatomical_count_constraints.ears: 1 large visible (side profile shows near ear)

animals.signature_features: single nose horn, armored skin plates simplified into folk panels, strong arched back
animals.signature_action: charging with focused power, head lowered horn forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: reclining camel-fashion, front legs folded beneath body
animals.anatomy_must_include: EXACTLY ONE nose horn (Indian one-horned rhinoceros has a single horn, NEVER two); armored skin folds across shoulders and haunches; rectangular ears
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: tikka medallion
NECK: folk collar band
ARMOR PLATES (most important): the rhino's armor folds rendered as ornate folk panels — shoulder plate with sun-medallion, flank plate with vine motif, haunch plate with rosette pattern
FOUR ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron bands
NOSE-HORN: single horn proportional (NOT two horns)
EARS: 2 small rectangular ears
TAIL: short with tufted tip

anatomical_count_constraints.horns_on_nose: 1 (single horn — Indian one-horned rhinoceros; NEVER 2 horns, that would be an African rhino)

animals.signature_features: enormous backward-sweeping crescent horns wider than the body, heavy shoulders, drooping dewlap simplified into folk panel, broad black muzzle
animals.signature_action: wading through marshland water with head lowered, horns sweeping wide, water dots scattering
animals.rest_pose_for_species: reclining in shallow water with body half-submerged, only the back, head and horns above the waterline (waterline rendered as a band of folk dots)
animals.anatomy_must_include: MASSIVE crescent horns that curve outward then back (the widest natural horns of any bovid, NEVER short stubby cattle horns); thicker heavier build than a domestic buffalo; both horns visible even in side profile due to their wide arc
animals.conservation_note: Truly wild progenitor of the domestic water buffalo; fewer than 4000 remain globally, with Kaziranga's herd as the largest stronghold.

animals.signature_features: leaner more elongated body than upland Bengal tiger, longer legs adapted to wading, body silhouette MUST carry 8-12 BOLD VERTICAL STRIPES preserved as flat folk-color bands (indigo/leaf-green/vermillion/gold) — the tiger stripe rhythm stays visible (NEVER replaced by florals), long tail held low and curved with banded folk pattern, mangrove leaf accents in negative space
animals.signature_action: stalking through mangrove shallows, body lowered, tail extended straight back, water dots scattering around the paws
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched on a folk-pattern mangrove root with forelimbs braced, tail draped, eyes alert toward the water
animals.anatomy_must_include: body filled with saturated walnut-brown NOT natural orange-and-black tiger photography; THE STRIPE PATTERN MUST READ AS STRIPES — 8-12 distinct vertical bands of flat folk color (indigo, leaf-green, vermillion, gold) across back/flank/shoulder; NOT scattered florals; leaner long-legged wading build distinct from upland Bengal tiger; almond eye not round cartoon eye; the result must be unambiguously a TIGER (vertical stripe rhythm visible)
animals.conservation_note: Only tiger population on Earth adapted to mangrove swamps and routinely entering saltwater; an apex predator of the Sundarbans delta shared between India and Bangladesh.

animals.signature_features: long horizontal body, broad V-shaped snout, scaled dorsal ridge translated into rhythmic folk dorsal panels of leaf-green and saffron, four short laterally-splayed legs, long muscular tail with paddle-like vertical scutes shown as decorative folk fins
animals.signature_action: lunging from water with body partially raised, jaws closed in dignified composure (NEVER open-mouth bite — keep mouth closed per folk-art register), water dots scattering around the body
animals.rest_pose_for_species: basking horizontally on a folk-pattern mudbank with all four legs splayed sideways, tail extended straight behind, head lifted alert
animals.anatomy_must_include: mouth CLOSED in default and rest poses (no exposed teeth, no open jaws — Madhubani register requires closed-mouth dignity); long body proportions with tail equal to or longer than body length; four legs laterally splayed not vertically planted like a mammal; scaled back NOT smooth — dorsal ridge always visible as rhythmic folk panels
animals.conservation_note: Largest living reptile on Earth; the Sundarbans and Bhitarkanika in India are critical strongholds. Heavily hunted historically and now recovering under protection.

animals.signature_features: shaggy black coat translated into rhythmic folk fur-strokes, cream V-shaped chest blaze rendered as a folk medallion, long pale muzzle with mobile lips, long curved cream-colored claws on all four feet, small ears mostly buried in shoulder fur
animals.signature_action: rearing up onto hind legs with forepaws extended outward in a defensive bipedal stance, claws splayed, muzzle lifted
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated on haunches with forepaws resting on the ground in front, muzzle relaxed, eye contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: cream V-shaped or U-shaped chest blaze MUST be visible as a folk medallion (defining feature of the species); long curved pale claws visible on all visible feet; shaggy long coat NOT a smooth-furred body; long mobile muzzle NOT a short bear-cub face; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: Insectivorous bear native only to the Indian subcontinent; the original 'bear' that inspired Mowgli's Baloo. Bandhavgarh and surrounding central Indian forests are core habitat.

animals.signature_features: massive three-tined antlers carried by males, shaggy mane around neck and throat translated into folk fringe, large mule-like ears, dark muzzle, longer body than chital
animals.signature_action: alert bellowing call with head and antlers raised high, neck stretched, mouth open in alarm bark
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying down with legs folded beneath body, head erect and alert, antlers held high
animals.anatomy_must_include: antlers have EXACTLY THREE tines per side (rusine pattern: brow tine plus a forked top — never the multi-tined coronet of a red deer or elk); shaggy neck fringe always visible on males; ears large and mule-like; no white spots (sambar are uniformly dark)
animals.conservation_note: Largest deer in India and the principal prey of the Bengal tiger across the central Indian forests.

animals.signature_features: short curved backward-arching horns carried by both sexes (heavier in males), stocky goat-like body, short coarse coat translated into folk linework, silver saddle patch on adult males rendered as a saffron-and-cream folk panel across the back
animals.signature_action: leaping confidently between cliff ledges with all four hooves clustered close beneath the body, head lifted
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert on a narrow rocky ledge with all four legs planted close together, head raised and looking outward
animals.anatomy_must_include: horns are SHORT and curved backward in a tight arc (never the long spiral of a markhor, never the straight horns of a blackbuck); stocky goat-like build NOT a slender antelope; both sexes carry horns
animals.conservation_note: State animal of Tamil Nadu; endemic to the Western Ghats, with the Periyar-Anamalai-Nilgiri belt holding nearly the entire global population.

animals.signature_features: silver mane halo as folk sunburst, dark face, ALMOND eye NOT round cartoon eye, leaf-tuft tail
animals.signature_action: leaping between branches, mane visible in motion
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated calmly, hands folded, tail curved
required_decoration_zones: SILVER MANE (signature): radiant folk sunburst around the dark face — cream-white and gold-yellow rays with vermillion tips
FACE: dark face with almond eye + closed mouth in serene contemplative expression (NEVER round cartoon eyes)
BODY: one chest medallion + a sparse leaf-vein band on the limbs (NOT all-over pattern)
TAIL: curved with leaf-shaped tuft visible at the tip
GROUND: small dotted ground line + peepal-leaf sprig beside the figure

anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 2 (frontal portrait) or 1 (side profile) — almond eyes NOT round cartoon eyes

animals.signature_features: enormous muscular shoulder hump translated into a saffron-and-vermillion folk crest, pale stocking-like markings on the lower legs rendered as cream folk anklets, sweeping pale-tipped horns curving outward, heavy dewlap simplified into folk panel, broad forehead with cream patch between the horns
animals.signature_action: standing tall on a folk ridge in proud territorial display, head raised, horns silhouetted against the sky
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing in shaded forest reclined in a half-rest, weight on three legs with one rear leg cocked, head dignified and alert
animals.anatomy_must_include: PROMINENT muscular shoulder hump that rises higher than the rump (defining feature — never a level back like cattle); cream/white stockings on all four lower legs (must be visible even in folk treatment); horns curve outward then upward with cream tips; this is NOT a domestic bison or an American buffalo
animals.conservation_note: Largest extant wild bovid on Earth, heavier than any African buffalo. Bandipur and Nagarhole in the Western Ghats hold a major share of the global population.

animals.signature_features: rosettes translated into SPARSE folk medallions across the body (eight to twelve total, NOT all-over photographic pattern), long heavy tail held in a relaxed S-curve usually dropping below the body, compact powerful build shorter and stockier than a tiger, almond eye, small rounded ears
animals.signature_action: crouched low on a folk-pattern tree branch with body pressed flat to the bark, tail draped over the side, eyes locked forward in ambush focus
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated upright with forelimbs straight, tail wrapped around the front of the body and tip lifted, head lifted in alert contemplation
animals.anatomy_must_include: body filled with saturated walnut-brown NOT natural tawny-yellow leopard photography; rosettes are SPARSE folk medallions (max 8-12 across the whole body, multi-colored interiors) NOT dense photographic spots; build is stockier and more compact than a tiger; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: Most widespread big cat in India, adaptable from Himalayan forests to dry scrub. Bandipur and the surrounding Western Ghats forests hold one of the densest populations in the country.

animals.signature_features: magnificent multi-tined antlers (the name literally means 'twelve-tined') translated into branching folk crowns, slender elegant build, pale belly rendered as a cream folk panel, long alert ears, dignified neck carriage
animals.signature_action: bellowing rutting call with head and antlers thrown back, neck arched, mouth open in proud trumpeting bark
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying down with legs folded neatly beneath the body, antlers held high, head alert toward the viewer
animals.anatomy_must_include: antlers carry MULTIPLE tines (twelve total is the namesake; minimum five tines per side must be visible in the folk treatment) — these are emphatically NOT the simple three-tined antlers of a sambar; build is more slender than sambar; no white spots on adults
animals.conservation_note: State animal of Madhya Pradesh; the hard-ground subspecies was saved from extinction at Kanha National Park, where the population recovered from fewer than 70 individuals in the 1970s.

animals.signature_features: bushy black-tipped tail (the diagnostic feature), large rounded ears with cream interior, short reddish coat rendered as warm walnut folk fill with saffron highlight panels, slender hound-like build, alert intelligent face with almond eye
animals.signature_action: running in pack-coordinated pursuit with body extended fully forward, both forelegs reaching forward together and both hind legs braced in a galloping crouch, bushy black tail streaming behind
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying alert with chest on the ground and forelegs extended, head raised and ears pricked, bushy tail curled around the rear
animals.anatomy_must_include: TAIL is bushy with a clearly BLACK tip (the most reliable field mark and an explicit folk anchor here); ears are large and rounded with pale cream interior; this is NOT a domestic dog and NOT a wolf — slimmer than a wolf, leggier than a fox, with hound-like proportions; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: Pack-living wild canid of the Asian forests; fewer than 2500 mature individuals globally. Kanha-Pench is one of the strongest remaining strongholds.

animals.signature_features: crested head, long elegant neck, tail with feather-eye motifs
animals.signature_action: tail fanned in full radial display, head turned profile
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched with tail trailing, head alert
required_decoration_zones: CREST: 3-5 fan feathers on top of head, each tipped with vermillion bead
FACE / EYE-REGION: indigo body with a white-and-vermillion folk pattern around the eye
NECK: long elegant neck rendered in deep indigo with a white-pearl folk-pattern collar

BODY / WING (most important): wing covered in DENSE multi-color folk panels — minimum 4 distinct zones each with floral medallions in indigo/leaf-green/saffron/vermillion + white-pearl accents; the wing must NOT be a flat indigo shape

TAIL (the signature): tail covered in 8-12 eye-spot ocellus motifs rendered as folk roundels (concentric circles in saffron/vermillion/leaf-green/indigo), each ocellus distinct — the tail is the most ornate part of the bird and MUST be densely patterned
FEET: vermillion + saffron anklets above the visible feet

anatomical_count_constraints.legs_visible: 2 (both legs on perch or ground line)
anatomical_count_constraints.crest_feathers: 3 to 5 (NOT one giant plume, NOT zero)

animals.signature_features: tall erectile crest mane running from head along back to tail, rendered as a saffron-and-cream folk plume; dark vertical stripes translated into rhythmic indigo folk panels across the flanks and legs; sloping back with shoulders higher than hips; long pointed ears; bushy tail
animals.signature_action: padding warily forward with mane fully raised and crest erect in defensive display, head low, tail held straight back
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying on the ground with forepaws extended forward, head resting low between the paws, mane settled, eyes alert toward the viewer
animals.anatomy_must_include: back SLOPES — shoulders distinctly higher than hips (defining hyena silhouette); long erectile DORSAL MANE/CREST from head to tail must be visible (this is the striped hyena, not the spotted hyena of Africa); stripes are vertical bars on the body NOT spots; pointed ears NOT rounded
animals.conservation_note: Only large hyena present in India; nocturnal, solitary, and locally threatened by persecution. Ranthambore and the Aravalli scrublands are important strongholds.

animals.signature_features: shorter scruffier mane than the African lion (ears always remain VISIBLE through the mane — diagnostic), prominent longitudinal belly skin fold running along the lower flank rendered as a folk panel line, tufted black-tipped tail, almond eye, broad powerful chest
animals.signature_action: standing in proud territorial pose on a folk ridge with head high, mane spread, tail flicked back, gaze fixed forward in regal command
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying sphinx-fashion with forelegs extended straight forward and head held high, tail draped around to the side, eyes contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: body filled with saturated walnut-brown NOT natural tawny-yellow lion photography; mane is SHORTER and scruffier than African lion AND THE EARS REMAIN VISIBLE through it (single most reliable field mark of Panthera leo persica); longitudinal BELLY SKIN FOLD must be present as a folk line along the lower flank; tail has tufted black tip
animals.conservation_note: The only wild lion population outside Africa, confined to Gir National Park and surrounding landscape in Gujarat; recovered from fewer than 20 individuals a century ago to over 600 today.

animals.signature_features: elegantly slender legs, slightly S-curved ringed horns carried by both sexes (longer in males), dark facial stripe from eye to muzzle rendered as a folk band, pale cream belly and rump patch as folk panels, large dark almond eye, white tail with black tip
animals.signature_action: leaping in a graceful arc with all four legs gathered close beneath the body in mid-stride, head and horns lifted forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying down with all four legs folded neatly beneath the body, head raised, horns lifted, eyes contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: horns are slender, gently S-curved with visible rings, and present on BOTH sexes (males longer than females) — emphatically NOT the long spiral horns of a blackbuck; dark eye-to-muzzle facial stripe must be visible; cream belly and rump patches as folk panels; build is smaller and more delicate than a blackbuck
animals.conservation_note: India's most widespread native gazelle, ranging across the arid northwest. Sacred and protected by the Bishnoi community of Rajasthan and Gujarat; the deserts of Gir-Saurashtra and the Thar are core habitat.

animals.signature_features: lustrous golden-cream coat translated as a saffron-and-gold folk halo over the indigo body fill, jet-black face with almond eyes, long tasseled tail held arched over the back, hands and feet rendered with clear digit count
animals.signature_action: leaping between folk-pattern branches with arms outstretched forward, tail streaming behind in an elegant arc, golden coat catching imaginary light
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated calmly on a folk-pattern branch with hands resting in the lap, tail draped down beside the body, head turned profile in contemplation
animals.anatomy_must_include: FACE is jet-black contrasted against the golden body — never a pale or pink face; coat color is golden-cream rendered as saffron/gold folk panels over the indigo body fill (NOT a uniformly indigo body); long arched tail held over the back NOT dragging behind; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: One of the rarest primates in the world; endemic to a tiny range in western Assam (India) and Bhutan, with Manas National Park as the only Indian stronghold.

animals.signature_features: smallest pig in the world translated into a compact stocky folk silhouette, short tapered snout, tiny erect ears, short coarse coat rendered with rhythmic indigo folk strokes, sparse short bristly mane along the back, very short tufted tail
animals.signature_action: trotting forward purposefully through tall folk-pattern grass with body low to the ground, snout extended, ears alert
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert in a small clearing of folk-pattern grass tussocks with all four short legs planted, snout raised slightly, eyes contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: SMALLEST wild pig in the world — the silhouette must read as a compact diminutive pig, NEVER the bulk of an Indian wild boar; snout is short and tapered NOT a long boar snout; no large tusks; ears are small and erect; build is dachshund-low to the ground
animals.conservation_note: Critically rare, fewer than 250 mature wild individuals remain, all in the Manas-Sonai-Rupai grasslands of Assam. The subject of an intensive captive-breeding and reintroduction program.

animals.signature_features: rosettes as SPARSE folk medallions (max 4-5 zones, NOT all-over pattern), flowing long tail held low, rounded ears
animals.signature_action: crouched on a Himalayan ledge, tail flicked
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated with tail wrapped around body for warmth
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: small tikka medallion
NECK: folk collar band

BODY: 4-5 SPARSE rosette medallions placed at specific zones (one shoulder, one flank, one hip, one base-of-tail) — vermillion-centered with saffron petals — NOT all-over rosette pattern (sparseness is the key)
BACK: one decorated saddle-band across the back
NECK BAND: gold-yellow folk band
FOUR ANKLETS: dotted folk bands at each leg joint
TAIL: long flowing held low, with banded folk pattern (NOT a fluffy realistic snow-leopard tail)

anatomical_count_constraints.legs_visible: 4 (all four legs as distinct outlines, near and far pair both fully drawn)

animals.signature_features: smoothly outward-then-backward curving horns carried by both sexes (much heavier in males), stocky mountain-sheep build, slate-blue-grey coat rendered as deep-indigo folk fill with cream belly panel, dark flank stripe translated into a vermillion folk band, cream lower legs as folk anklets, short ears
animals.signature_action: leaping across a vertical rocky cliff face with all four hooves gathered close beneath the body, head and horns thrown forward, mid-flight against the cliff
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert on a high folk-pattern rock ledge with all four legs braced close together, head turned profile to look outward across the valley
animals.anatomy_must_include: horns curve OUTWARD and then BACKWARD in a smooth heavy arc (heavier and more outward-swept in males) — NEVER the tight backward arc of a Nilgiri tahr and NEVER the long spirals of a markhor; coat is slate-blue-grey (indigo in folk register); dark flank stripe must be visible as a band separating the dark back from cream belly; both sexes carry horns; stocky goat-sheep build not a slender antelope
animals.conservation_note: Principal prey species of the snow leopard across the Indian high-Himalayas; Hemis and the surrounding Ladakh ranges hold dense populations.

animals.signature_features: tallest flying bird on Earth — extraordinarily long legs and neck rendered as graceful folk verticals, bare red skin of head and upper neck translated into a vermillion folk crown panel, grey body fill rendered as deep-indigo, long straight pointed bill, bustle of plumes drooping over the tail
animals.signature_action: courtship dance with both wings spread wide and head thrown back, beak open in a trumpeting unison call, body lifted on tiptoes
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing in shallow folk-pattern wetland water on one long leg with the other leg folded up beneath the body, neck curved into an S, head turned profile
animals.anatomy_must_include: TALLEST flying bird in the world — proportions must emphasize extreme leg and neck length (legs longer than the body is tall); BARE RED skin of head and upper neck must be rendered as a vermillion folk crown/throat panel (defining field mark); body is grey-indigo NOT white (this is not a flamingo, not a stork, not a Siberian crane); bustle of inner secondary plumes drooping over the tail
animals.conservation_note: Sacred symbol of marital fidelity across Indian folk tradition (Sarus pairs mate for life); Keoladeo Ghana in Bharatpur is the most famous Indian breeding site.

animals.signature_features: long downcurved yellow bill rendered as a saffron folk wedge, bare orange-yellow face skin translated into a saffron folk facial panel, mainly white body translated as indigo body fill with cream chest panel, pink tertial flight feathers translated into vermillion folk panels along the lower back and rump, long pink legs rendered as vermillion folk verticals
animals.signature_action: wings half-spread in display with head lifted and bill pointed slightly upward, one leg lifted forward in a courtship step, pink tertials fanned
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on one folk-pattern wetland leg in shallow water, other leg folded under, neck tucked into a calm S-curve, bill resting against chest, eye contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: BILL is long, heavy, DOWNCURVED and yellow (saffron folk wedge) — NEVER the straight bill of a sarus crane or the upturned bill of an avocet; pink tertial flight feathers are a defining field mark and MUST appear as vermillion folk panels along the lower back/rump; bare yellow-orange face skin as a saffron folk facial panel; long pink legs
animals.conservation_note: Colonial nesting stork dependent on shallow freshwater wetlands; Keoladeo Ghana hosts iconic mass-breeding heronries.

animals.signature_features: powerful muscular shoulders higher than the hips giving a sloping silhouette, prominent erect dorsal MANE/CREST of stiff bristles running from head along the back rendered as a saffron-and-vermillion folk plume, long tapered snout, short upward-curving tusks of the male, small alert ears, short tufted tail
animals.signature_action: charging forward at full power with body low and head lowered, mane fully erect in a dorsal crest, tusks angled forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on guard with all four legs planted, snout lifted slightly, mane partly raised along the back, eye alert toward the viewer
animals.anatomy_must_include: ERECT DORSAL MANE/CREST of stiff bristles along the head and back — this is the cristatus subspecies and the crest is the defining feature versus European wild boar; shoulders distinctly higher than hips giving a sloping back; long tapered snout NOT a domestic pig snout; tusks short and upward-curving in males only; this is emphatically NOT a pygmy hog (much larger and more powerful)
animals.conservation_note: Adaptable ancestor of the domestic pig; an important prey species for tigers and leopards. Tadoba-Andhari supports a thriving boar population.

animals.signature_features: overlapping keratin scales covering the entire body and tail translated into rhythmic interlocking folk panels (saffron, leaf-green, vermillion), small conical head with a long tubular snout, no visible external ears, long heavy prehensile tail thicker than the body width, strong curved digging claws on the forelimbs
animals.signature_action: walking forward on hind legs with forelimbs folded close to the chest and the long heavy tail used as a counterbalance, scales catching imaginary light
animals.rest_pose_for_species: curled into a tight defensive ball with the tail wrapped over the head and body, only the scaled outer surface visible — rendered as a perfect folk medallion of interlocking scale panels
animals.anatomy_must_include: ENTIRE BODY and TAIL covered in overlapping scales — translated into rhythmic interlocking folk panels; tail is THICK AND HEAVY (thicker than the body width — diagnostic of crassicaudata, 'thick-tailed'); long tubular snout NOT a rodent face; no visible external ears; strong forelimb claws; this is not an aardvark and not an armadillo
animals.conservation_note: Severely threatened by illegal trade for scales and meat; pangolins are the most-trafficked wild mammals on Earth. Tadoba-Andhari is part of their central-Indian forest range.

animals.signature_features: extraordinarily long bushy tail equal to or longer than the body rendered as a flowing saffron-and-vermillion folk plume, cream belly and chest as a folk panel, tufted ears, large dark almond eye, strong curved climbing claws on all four feet
animals.signature_action: leaping between folk-pattern canopy branches with body fully extended, long bushy tail streaming behind as a counterbalance, forelimbs reaching forward toward the next branch
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated upright on a folk-pattern branch with the long bushy tail curled up behind the back, forepaws holding a folk-pattern fruit at the chest, head turned profile
animals.anatomy_must_include: TAIL is EXTREMELY long and bushy (equal to or longer than the body — defining feature, this is one of the largest squirrels on Earth); the multi-tone fur (deep maroon/black back, rust shoulders, cream belly) is translated into multi-color Madhubani folk panels rather than a uniformly indigo body — ensure visible saffron/vermillion zones on the back-and-tail with a cream belly panel; large dark almond eye not round cartoon eye; tufted ears
animals.conservation_note: State animal of Maharashtra; emblematic of intact Western Ghats canopy. Anamalai's evergreen forests are core habitat.

animals.signature_features: glossy black coat rendered as deep-indigo folk fill, golden-brown mane around the head and shoulders translated into a saffron-and-gold folk halo, long tail held in a graceful arc, dark face with almond eyes, hands and feet with clear digit count
animals.signature_action: leaping powerfully between folk-pattern canopy branches with both arms extended forward and tail streaming straight back in dynamic balance
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated calmly on a folk-pattern branch with hands resting on knees, tail draped down and curled at the tip, head turned profile in contemplation
animals.anatomy_must_include: GOLDEN-BROWN mane around the head and shoulders MUST be rendered as a saffron-and-gold folk halo over the indigo body — this is the diagnostic feature of johnii versus other langurs; dark face (not pink); long tail held in graceful arc NOT dragging on the ground; almond eye not round cartoon eye; this is NOT the grey common langur (Semnopithecus entellus)
animals.conservation_note: Endemic to the southern Western Ghats; the dark forest counterpart to the more familiar grey Hanuman langur. Anamalai and the surrounding shola-grassland forests are critical habitat.

animals.signature_features: largest antelope in Asia, sloping back with shoulders higher than hips, short straight forward-pointing horns carried only by the male, prominent throat mane rendered as a saffron-and-cream folk fringe, white facial spots and throat patch translated into cream folk medallions, cream stockings on the lower legs as folk anklets, short black mane along the upper neck
animals.signature_action: standing in proud territorial display on a folk ridge with head held high, throat mane prominent, horns silhouetted forward, one foreleg slightly advanced
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying down with legs folded neatly beneath the body, head erect and alert, horns lifted, eyes contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: back SLOPES — shoulders higher than hips (defining nilgai silhouette, unlike most antelopes); horns are SHORT, STRAIGHT and forward-pointing, carried only by the male (females are hornless) — NEVER the long spirals of a blackbuck or the ringed S-curve of a chinkara; pronounced throat mane / 'pendant' beard on males; white facial spots and throat patch as cream folk medallions
animals.conservation_note: Largest antelope on the Asian continent; revered in Hindu folk tradition (the name literally means 'blue bull'). Sariska and the Aravalli landscape hold large populations.

animals.signature_features: elongated low-slung body with short legs, grizzled grey coat rendered as deep-indigo folk fill with rhythmic cream and saffron speckle-panels, long tapered tail nearly equal to body length with a contrasting paler tip, small rounded ears low on the head, sharp pointed snout, almond eye with intelligent alert expression
animals.signature_action: rearing up on hind legs in classic mongoose-versus-cobra alert pose, forepaws raised, head and tail held high, body coiled with potential energy
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated on haunches with forepaws held in front of the chest, long tail draped behind, head turned profile in alert contemplation
animals.anatomy_must_include: ELONGATED low-slung body proportions with short legs (this is NOT a cat, NOT a squirrel) — the silhouette must read as a slender mustelid-like mongoose; long tapered tail nearly the body's length with a paler tip; sharp pointed snout; grizzled coat translated into rhythmic speckle-panels NOT smooth fill; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: Iconic snake-fighter of Indian folk tradition — the Rikki-Tikki-Tavi of Kipling. Widespread across India; Sariska is representative of its dry-forest habitat.

animals.signature_features: distinctive white spots in rows across the back and flanks translated into cream-and-saffron folk medallions arranged in graceful rows over the indigo body, three-tined lyre-shaped antlers carried by males, slender graceful build, cream belly and throat panels, dark facial stripe rendered as a folk band
animals.signature_action: leaping in a graceful arc with all four legs gathered close beneath the body, head and antlers held forward, mid-flight
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying down with legs folded neatly beneath the body, head raised and alert, antlers lifted, eyes contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: WHITE SPOTS retained throughout adulthood (most Indian deer lose spots; chital keep them for life) — rendered as cream-and-saffron folk medallions in rows; antlers are LYRE-SHAPED with EXACTLY THREE tines per side (brow tine plus a forked top — same tine count as sambar, but more slender and lyre-shaped); slender graceful build distinct from the bulkier sambar; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: The most abundant deer in India and the principal tiger prey across the central Indian forests; Pench's dry deciduous woodlands hold dense herds.

animals.signature_features: long bushy tail with a distinctive BLACK tip, large pointed ears with cream interior rendered as folk panels, slender delicate build smaller than a red fox, pointed muzzle, almond eye with intelligent expression, sandy-grey coat translated into warm walnut folk fill with saffron and vermillion accent panels
animals.signature_action: trotting forward with body extended and bushy tail held straight out horizontally behind, head alert and forward, ears pricked
animals.rest_pose_for_species: lying curled with the long bushy tail wrapped around the body and over the muzzle, head resting on forepaws, eye alert toward the viewer
animals.anatomy_must_include: TAIL is bushy with a clear BLACK TIP (single most reliable field mark of Vulpes bengalensis); smaller and more delicate than a European red fox; large pointed ears with cream interior; pointed muzzle; this is NOT a dhole (much smaller) and NOT a domestic dog; almond eye not round cartoon eye
animals.conservation_note: Endemic to the Indian subcontinent; an adaptable small carnivore of open grassland and scrub. Pench's edges and adjacent agro-pastoral landscapes hold healthy populations.

animals.signature_features: russet-red coat translated as warm walnut folk fill with rich vermillion and saffron accent panels, long bushy ringed tail rendered as alternating cream and walnut folk bands, white facial markings (cheeks, brows, muzzle) as cream folk medallions, dark eye patches as indigo folk panels, large pointed ears with cream interior, black legs and underparts as indigo folk panels
animals.signature_action: balanced upright on a folk-pattern bamboo branch with forepaws raised in defensive 'kung-fu' stance, tail extended for balance, head alert
animals.rest_pose_for_species: curled tightly on a folk-pattern branch with the long ringed tail wrapped completely around the body covering the face partly, eye peeking out alert
animals.anatomy_must_include: TAIL is LONG and BUSHY with CLEAR ALTERNATING RINGS (rendered as folk bands of cream and walnut alternately — this is the defining field mark); WHITE FACIAL MARKINGS (cheeks, brows, muzzle) MUST be present as cream folk medallions; coat color is russet-red NOT brown — translated as warm walnut with vermillion/saffron accents over the underlying walnut fill; this is NOT a raccoon, NOT a fox, and definitely NOT the unrelated giant panda; small body the size of a large house cat
animals.conservation_note: First-described 'panda' (decades before the giant panda); endemic to the eastern Himalayas. Namdapha National Park in Arunachal Pradesh is a stronghold of the Indian range.

animals.signature_features: extraordinarily long arms (longer than the legs) translated into elongated folk verticals, no tail (true ape), white eyebrow band across the dark face rendered as a prominent cream folk band, hands and feet with five clear digits, hooked grasping fingers, almond eye, dignified face
animals.signature_action: brachiating in mid-swing between folk-pattern canopy branches with both arms fully extended overhead reaching forward, body extended below, legs trailing
animals.rest_pose_for_species: seated upright on a folk-pattern branch with the long arms folded across the chest or resting on knees, no tail, head turned profile in contemplation
animals.anatomy_must_include: ARMS are SIGNIFICANTLY LONGER than the legs (defining feature of all gibbons — apes, not monkeys); NO TAIL (true ape — this is the only ape native to India); WHITE EYEBROW BAND across the dark face MUST be visible as a cream folk band (defining field mark of Hoolock); five clearly drawn digits on hands and feet; almond eye not round cartoon eye; this is NOT a langur and NOT a macaque
animals.conservation_note: The only ape native to India; threatened by habitat fragmentation. Namdapha National Park and the forests of northeast India are critical strongholds.

animals.signature_features: BLUNT ROUNDED head with no beak (the defining feature versus other dolphins), small low triangular dorsal fin set far back on the body, broad rounded flippers, smiling slit mouth, body curve rendered with saffron and cream folk panels indicating water motion, water dots as folk ground-mark instead of land
animals.signature_action: leaping in a graceful arc above the waterline of a folk-pattern lagoon, body curved upward, flippers extended, water dots scattering below
animals.rest_pose_for_species: suspended motionless horizontal in deep water with body fully extended straight, flippers relaxed at sides, eye calm contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: HEAD is BLUNT and ROUNDED with NO BEAK — emphatically NOT the long pointed rostrum of an oceanic dolphin (this is the single defining field mark of Orcaella); dorsal fin is SMALL, TRIANGULAR, and set FAR BACK on the body (NEVER the tall curved dorsal of a bottlenose); broad rounded flippers; gentle slit smile (closed mouth); body fill is deep-indigo with white spot-and-band constellations as Madhubani dot-and-circle folk motifs; no ocean background, no scenery — figure isolated on the cream/black tee field with only a folk water-dot ground mark
animals.conservation_note: Chilika Lake on the Odisha coast holds the largest known population of Irrawaddy dolphins on Earth; the species is also found in select estuaries and rivers across South and Southeast Asia.

animals.signature_features: extraordinarily long graceful S-curved neck, very long stilt-like legs rendered as vermillion folk verticals, distinctive DOWNWARD-BENT pink bill with black tip translated into a saffron-and-indigo folk wedge, pale pink body translated as indigo body fill with vermillion and saffron accent panels on the wings, black flight feathers as indigo folk panels visible when wings spread
animals.signature_action: wings half-spread in display with head dipped low and neck curved into a deep S, one long leg lifted forward, pink-and-black wings showing the diagnostic color contrast
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on ONE long folk-pattern leg in shallow folk water, the other leg folded up under the body, neck curled gracefully and head tucked back against the body, eye contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: BILL is DOWNWARD-BENT (sharply kinked roughly halfway along its length with a BLACK tip) — defining field mark across all flamingo species and rendered here as a saffron-and-indigo folk wedge; extremely long stilt-like legs and long S-curved neck; PINK plumage rendered as vermillion/saffron folk accent panels over the indigo body (NOT a uniformly indigo bird); often shown standing on ONE leg
animals.conservation_note: Chilika Lake is one of the largest wintering grounds in India, hosting tens of thousands of greater flamingos every year alongside its lesser flamingos.

animals.signature_features: enormous bright yellow CASQUE on top of the massive curved yellow bill rendered as a saffron-and-gold folk crown, jet-black body rendered as forest-teal folk fill with cream and saffron accent panels, white wing bar and white tail with a black band translated into rhythmic folk panels, large body, broad wings
animals.signature_action: in flight with both massive wings fully spread sideways, head and casque held forward, tail fanned, the iconic silhouette of the rainforest canopy
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched on a folk-pattern canopy branch with body upright, massive bill and casque held forward, wings folded against the body, tail trailing down, eye contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: ENORMOUS yellow CASQUE on top of the bill is the single defining field mark of Buceros bicornis — MUST be present as a prominent saffron-and-gold folk crown sitting on top of an equally massive curved yellow bill; jet-black body translated as forest-teal folk fill (NOT indigo — explicitly forest-teal anchor for this forest-canopy species); WHITE tail with a single BLACK band visible as folk panels; white wing-bar visible when wings spread; the bill-and-casque assembly is roughly a third of total body length
animals.conservation_note: State bird of Kerala (and of Arunachal Pradesh); the largest hornbill in the Indian subcontinent. Silent Valley's pristine rainforest canopy is a stronghold.

animals.display_name: Indian King Cobra (Silent Valley Forest Race)
animals.signature_features: hood with spectacle pattern, S-curve body, almond eyes, mouth CLOSED default
animals.signature_action: rearing with hood fully spread, head high
animals.rest_pose_for_species: coiled with hood lowered, head resting on coils
required_decoration_zones: HOOD (most important): the spread hood covered in dense Madhubani folk patterns — central mandala-style medallion + spectacle-pattern eye-spots + folk floral border around the edge
BODY: serpent coils covered in alternating folk panels — vermillion + saffron bands, leaf-green vine motifs, dot patterns, NOT plain forest-teal
BELLY-SCALES: rendered as alternating cream and indigo horizontal bands along the belly side of the coils
FRAME: optional decorative floral border in the four corners of the canvas (matches the kohbar wedding-diagram convention)

anatomical_count_constraints.tongue: either NOT visible (mouth closed, default) OR exactly ONE forked tongue with a single split-tip at the very end — the tongue is ONE structure that forks at the tip only, NOT two separate tongues, NOT a Y-shape from the mouth, NOT two parallel tongues
anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 2 (frontal hood view shows both) or 1 (side profile)
anatomical_count_constraints.hood_lobes: 2 (one on each side of the head, forming the spread hood)

animals.signature_features: broad flat head, gentle slit mouth (not a smile), white spot constellations across the back translated into Madhubani dot-and-circle motifs as folk decoration, long body in a graceful curve, large pectoral fins, distinctive heterocercal caudal fin (upper lobe longer than lower)
animals.signature_action: gliding peacefully through deep water with mouth slightly open as a filter feeder, body in a wide graceful curve
animals.rest_pose_for_species: suspended motionless in deep water, body fully extended straight, fins relaxed at sides
animals.anatomy_must_include: broad flat head (NOT pointed shark snout); small eye positioned at front of head; white spot constellations rendered as folk dot patterns NOT as photographic markings; no scenery, no ocean background, no bubbles — the figure is isolated on the cream/black tee field
animals.conservation_note: The largest fish in the world; gentle filter feeder. Found in Indian waters off the Gujarat coast (Saurashtra). Protected in India under the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 since 2001.

animals.signature_features: the only Indian bird with a LOWER mandible distinctly LONGER than the upper — bright orange bill with the lower jaw extended forward rendered as a striking saffron-and-vermillion folk wedge, black cap rendered as deep-indigo folk panel, white forehead and underparts as cream folk panels, long pointed wings, short orange-red legs
animals.signature_action: skimming low over folk-pattern water with the long lower mandible slicing the surface in a horizontal line, wings angled upward, water dots scattering along the wake
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on a folk-pattern sandbar with both short legs planted, wings folded back, head facing forward, lower mandible resting along the upper, eye contemplative
animals.anatomy_must_include: LOWER MANDIBLE is DISTINCTLY LONGER than the upper mandible — the unique defining field mark of skimmers (Rynchopidae); bill is bright orange with the lower jaw clearly extended forward, rendered as a saffron-and-vermillion folk wedge; black cap above, white forehead and underparts below; long pointed gull-like wings; short orange legs; this is NOT a tern and NOT a gull
animals.conservation_note: One of the most rapidly declining waterbirds in South Asia; coastal Gujarat (including the Marine National Park region) and central Indian rivers are key habitat. Fewer than 4000 mature birds remain.

animals.signature_features: elegant spiral horns rising in graceful corkscrew curves, sharply two-toned coat (deep upper body, white belly + cheek + chin), slender muscular neck, almond eye with calm contemplative gaze, slim tapered tail
animals.signature_action: leaping mid-stride with all four legs off the ground in the species' iconic 'pronk' bound, horns held high, head facing forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: kneeling on forelegs with hindquarters tucked under, horns held upright, eye looking forward in repose
animals.anatomy_must_include: spiral corkscrew horns (NOT straight, NOT branching like deer antlers) rising up and outward in a graceful double-twist; sharp white-on-indigo two-tone pattern with the white cheek patch + chin + belly clearly delineated against the indigo upper body as solid flat folk shapes; almond eye not round cartoon eye; this is an antelope NOT a deer
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: tikka medallion between the horns
NECK: folk collar in vermillion + saffron
BODY INTERIOR: large sun-mandala medallion on the flank + sparse leaf-vein bands across the back (matches pass_examples/blackbuck_v3 style)
WHITE CHEEK PATCH: rendered as flat folk shape (cream/white), sharply delineated against the indigo upper body
WHITE BELLY: similar flat folk treatment
FOUR ANKLETS: vermillion bands at each leg joint
TAIL: short with white underside as folk panel

anatomical_count_constraints.horns: 2 spiral corkscrew horns rising up and outward (not branching like deer antlers; NOT straight)
anatomical_count_constraints.white_areas: cheek + chin + belly clearly delineated as flat cream folk shapes

animals.signature_features: curved trunk with visible ring lines, large rounded ear (smaller than African elephant), small males-only tusks proportional and not exaggerated, almond eye, broad triangular shoulder, sturdy pillar legs. Nagarhole is known for the largest tuskers in southern India crossing the Kabini river — the bull elephant silhouette should read as ROBUST and MATURE, not juvenile.
animals.signature_action: trumpeting trunk raised high with mouth open in call, body striding forward, water droplets scattering
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing relaxed in shade with trunk curled, one rear leg lifted in characteristic at-rest pose
animals.anatomy_must_include: 1 trunk (curved), 4 pillar legs all visible, large fan-like ear in side profile, small proportional tusks (not exaggerated giant tusks, not absent), almond eye not round cartoon eye. Elephant must be unambiguously ASIAN not African (smaller rounded ears, twin-domed forehead).
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: vermillion + saffron tikka medallion between the eyes
EAR: large folk-pattern panel filling the visible ear with leaf-vein motifs and dot bands in vermillion + leaf-green
NECK: multi-color folk collar (indigo / saffron / vermillion / leaf-green)
BODY: ornate decorated saddle blanket with central lotus medallion + leaf-vein border, vermillion + saffron palette
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron + cream folk bands at each ankle
TUSKS: 2 small visible tusks (proportional), tip-decorated with simple gold-leaf accent
TAIL: short with folk-tufted tip

anatomical_count_constraints.ears: 1 large visible (side profile shows near ear)

animals.conservation_note: Nagarhole holds one of the highest Asian elephant densities in India (~1300 individuals) and is contiguous with Bandipur, Mudumalai, and Wayanad as the Nilgiri Biosphere — the largest elephant landscape in South Asia.

animals.signature_features: russet-red coat (NOT brown wolf, NOT yellow jackal), white chest blaze, bushy BLACK-TIPPED tail (the defining diagnostic), rounded fox-like ears, slim athletic body. The black tail tip is the single most identifying feature — without it the rendering reads as a generic canine.
animals.signature_action: running at full gait with body extended low, tail streaming horizontal, ears pinned back, head forward
animals.rest_pose_for_species: alert seated upright with forelimbs straight, ears erect and forward, tail wrapped around feet
animals.anatomy_must_include: russet-red walnut-brown body fill NOT generic gray/brown wolf coloring; BUSHY tail with DISTINCT BLACK TIP (mandatory diagnostic); white chest blaze; rounded ears (not pointed wolf ears); almond eye not round cartoon eye. The result must be unambiguously a DHOLE (Asian wild dog) and not a generic wolf, fox, or jackal.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: small vermillion tikka medallion between the eyes
NECK: folk collar band with chest blaze rendered as a folk-cream panel
BODY: ornate flank rosettes and shoulder medallions in saffron + vermillion + leaf-green
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + leaf-green folk bands at each leg joint
TAIL: black-tipped tail with the body portion patterned in folk rings, the tip remaining solid black as the diagnostic anchor
EAR INTERIOR: folk-dot pattern in vermillion + saffron

anatomical_count_constraints.mouth: closed (no tongue, no fangs in default poses)

animals.conservation_note: Nagarhole + Bandipur landscape is one of the world's most important dhole strongholds — fewer than 2500 mature individuals globally; pack-hunting apex predator that shares the niche of African painted dogs but is endemic to Asia.

animals.signature_features: male is brilliant ULTRAMARINE BLUE on back, crown, and tail-coverts CONTRASTING with VELVET-BLACK underparts (face, throat, breast, belly, wings) — this two-tone blue-and-black is the unmistakable diagnostic; thick black bill; red iris ring; stocky perching posture. Female is duller blue-green throughout (less commonly rendered).
animals.signature_action: perched on a fruiting fig branch with crest slightly raised and head turned to display the brilliant blue contrast against black
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched compact on a horizontal branch with feet folded, tail slightly fanned, body settled
animals.anatomy_must_include: brilliant BLUE upper parts ABRUPTLY meeting BLACK lower parts (sharp two-tone boundary along the flank-line — not a gradient); proportional stout black bill; red eye ring; both legs visible on the perch; the bird MUST read as a perched canopy frugivore, not a flycatcher or songbird with washed coloring.
required_decoration_zones: CROWN: ultramarine-blue folk crest with small saffron + vermillion dot accents
BACK: folk-pattern blue panel with leaf-vein motifs in indigo
BREAST + BELLY (the black zone): the black is preserved as the body anchor with vermillion + saffron rosette accents at the chest center only — do NOT overdecorate the velvet black
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + leaf-green
TAIL: short folk bars with ultramarine + saffron tips
FEET: both perched, with vermillion ankle bands

anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 1 in side profile (with red iris ring)

animals.conservation_note: Western Ghats endemic canopy fruit-eater; Nagarhole's mixed deciduous forest with fig trees is prime habitat. Considered a sentinel species for healthy primary-forest canopies.

animals.signature_features: crow-sized black body with brilliant CRIMSON CREST (full erect in males; smaller in females), pure WHITE BELLY contrasting with the all-black back/wings, strong chisel-shaped pale bill, stiff pointed tail used as a brace against tree trunks. The CRIMSON CREST + WHITE BELLY + BLACK BODY combination is unmistakable.
animals.signature_action: clinging vertically to a tree trunk in characteristic woodpecker brace — feet gripping bark, stiff tail pressed down for support, head drawn back ready to strike
animals.rest_pose_for_species: clinging upright on a vertical trunk with body parallel to the bark, head turned to one side
animals.anatomy_must_include: BRILLIANT CRIMSON CREST on top of head (not just red — saturated vermillion-crimson); WHITE BELLY clearly visible against the body's black-indigo (mandatory contrast); chisel pale bill (NOT a curved or hooked bill); 2 feet gripping vertical bark in zygodactyl arrangement (2 toes forward, 2 back); stiff pointed tail tip used as brace.
required_decoration_zones: CREST: full erect crimson crest with small saffron + vermillion tip accents
FACE: black with a small vermillion folk-dot at the eye and around the bill base
BACK + WINGS: black-indigo with folk feather bars in indigo + leaf-green
WHITE BELLY: the white is preserved as a cream-paper panel with minimal vermillion folk-dot border (do NOT overdecorate the white)
TAIL: stiff folk-bar pattern with leaf-vein motifs
FEET: zygodactyl grip rendered as folk pattern at the trunk attachment
animals.conservation_note: Largest woodpecker in the Indian subcontinent; declining due to old-growth forest loss. Nagarhole's mature dipterocarp forests host one of the densest populations in southern India.

animals.signature_features: slate-blue-gray coat (the 'blue' in the name) with characteristic WHITE BELLY and white flanks; males have HEAVY BACKWARD-CURVING SMOOTH HORNS that sweep outward and then back; females have small upright spike horns. Black stripe down each flank separating the gray back from the white belly. Compact muscular body adapted to high-altitude cliffs.
animals.signature_action: leaping across a rocky cliff with all four feet airborne, head and horns leading, body extended in mid-bound
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert on a rocky outcrop with all four feet planted, head turned to scan, horns visible in profile
animals.anatomy_must_include: HEAVY backward-curving horns (males only — sweep outward then back, like ibex but smaller and smoother); SLATE-BLUE-GRAY coat NOT plain brown or tan; WHITE BELLY clearly demarcated; BLACK FLANK STRIPE separating gray back from white belly (mandatory); 4 legs all visible; the result must read as BHARAL (bluish-gray cliff-dweller) not generic sheep or generic ibex.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: vermillion tikka between the horns
HORNS: gold-leaf accent along the ridge with folk-band rings at base
NECK: folk collar with leaf-vein motifs in indigo + leaf-green
BODY: ornate flank rosettes in vermillion + saffron, the black flank stripe rendered as a folk-pattern band, the white belly preserved as a cream panel with vermillion border
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron folk bands
TAIL: short with folk-tufted tip

anatomical_count_constraints.horns: 2 backward-curving (males) or 2 small upright (females) — default males

animals.conservation_note: Primary prey of the snow leopard across the Himalayan range; the Great Himalayan National Park hosts one of the densest bharal populations in Himachal Pradesh, supporting the park's resident snow leopards.

animals.signature_features: PALE CREAM-BUFF colored shaggy coat (lighter than other brown bear subspecies — sometimes called 'isabelline brown bear'), prominent shoulder hump, broad heavy head, small rounded ears, long claws visible on forepaws. The unusually PALE color is the key diagnostic — much lighter than Himalayan black bear (which is solid black with white chest crescent).
animals.signature_action: rearing up on hind legs with forepaws raised forward, head tilted up, the full pale shaggy coat visible
animals.rest_pose_for_species: reclining on the ground with body curled, head resting on forepaws, hump prominent
animals.anatomy_must_include: PALE CREAM-BUFF walnut-brown body fill (NOT dark chocolate brown, NOT black — this is the LIGHTEST brown bear subspecies); shaggy coat texture rendered as folk-line pattern; PROMINENT SHOULDER HUMP; long forepaw claws visible; small rounded ears; the result must read as HIMALAYAN BROWN BEAR not Himalayan black bear, not grizzly, not American black bear.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: vermillion tikka medallion between the small ears
SHOULDER HUMP: ornate folk medallion in vermillion + saffron at the hump peak
NECK: folk collar in indigo + leaf-green
BODY: shaggy coat rendered as parallel folk-line texture across back and flanks, with ornament zones at hip and shoulder
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron folk bands
CLAWS: gold-leaf accent on forepaw claws
TAIL: short stubby with folk-cream tip
animals.conservation_note: One of India's most endangered large mammals — fewer than 200 individuals remain in the western Himalaya. The Great Himalayan National Park is one of only 5-6 viable populations remaining, alongside Hemis and Nanda Devi.

animals.signature_features: male is spectacular CRIMSON BODY covered in WHITE SPOTS each ringed with BLACK (the defining pattern), bright BLUE FACIAL SKIN around the eye and throat, BLUE GULAR WATTLE (throat-skin lappet that inflates during courtship), short black tail. Female is dull mottled brown for camouflage. The male's crimson-with-white-spots is unmistakable and the rarest 'jewel pheasant' of the western Himalaya.
animals.signature_action: male in courtship display with gular wattle fully inflated and exposed, head tilted forward, white-spotted breast puffed
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on the forest floor with body horizontal, head turned to display blue facial skin in profile
animals.anatomy_must_include: CRIMSON body fill with WHITE SPOTS each ringed with BLACK (the ringed-spot pattern is THE diagnostic — never plain spots, never plain crimson); BLUE FACIAL SKIN around eye; BLUE GULAR WATTLE at throat; both feet visible on ground; short tail not long; the result must read as WESTERN TRAGOPAN not satyr tragopan (which has different head pattern) and not any common pheasant.
required_decoration_zones: CROWN: black with small crimson + saffron folk-dot accents
FACE: blue facial skin preserved as a clean blue panel with vermillion eye ring
GULAR WATTLE: blue lappet rendered as a folk-blue panel with leaf-vein motifs

BREAST + BODY: the crimson body covered in WHITE-ON-BLACK-RING dots translated to folk-art language as ornament dots — each a small white circle with a folk-black border ring, scattered rhythmically across the entire body
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + crimson + black
TAIL: short black with folk-bar tip
FEET: both visible with vermillion ankle bands

anatomical_count_constraints.gular_wattle: 1 (the inflatable throat skin — must be visible in display pose)

animals.conservation_note: Endemic to the western Himalaya; fewer than 5000 mature individuals. The Great Himalayan National Park's UNESCO inscription (2014) specifically cites the western tragopan as a flagship species — it survives almost nowhere else in India.

animals.signature_features: long-tailed pheasant with pale BUFF-AND-DARK-BROWN BARRED plumage, distinctive forward-bending CREST of dark feathers, slim athletic build, very LONG TAIL streaming behind (in males up to 18 inches), pink-red facial skin around the eye. Quieter coloring than other Himalayan pheasants — its character is in the LONG TAIL and CREST, not bright color.
animals.signature_action: running uphill through grass with body lowered, long tail streaming behind, crest swept back in motion
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert on a grassy slope with long tail draped behind, crest erect, head turned
animals.anatomy_must_include: VERY LONG TAIL (about as long as the body itself — must be prominent and streaming, not stubby); FORWARD-BENDING CREST of dark feathers; PALE-BUFF-AND-BROWN BARRED body pattern (NOT solid brown, NOT crimson); pink-red facial skin around eye; both feet visible; the result must read as CHEER PHEASANT (long-tailed runner) not a common partridge or generic pheasant.
required_decoration_zones: CREST: dark folk-feather crest with leaf-vein motifs
FACE: pink-red facial skin rendered as a clean folk panel with vermillion eye ring
BREAST: folk-bar pattern in buff + indigo with rhythmic dot accents
BODY: barred pattern translated as folk-stripes in indigo + saffron alternating across back and flanks
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + leaf-green
LONG TAIL: the very long tail rendered as a sequence of folk-pattern bands running from base to tip — the tail length is the defining visual element
FEET: both visible with vermillion ankle bands
animals.conservation_note: Highly threatened Himalayan endemic; declined sharply due to grass-slope habitat loss and hunting. The Great Himalayan National Park is one of the strongest remaining populations in Himachal Pradesh.

animals.signature_features: RUSSET-RED coat (warm coppery-red, not orange, not brown), distinctive RINGED BUSHY TAIL (alternating russet and pale cream bands), WHITE FACE MASK with russet eye-streaks (tear-line markings from eyes down the cheeks), BLACK LEGS contrasting against the russet body, large rounded ears with white fur insides. Cat-sized but moves with arboreal agility.
animals.signature_action: perched on a bamboo branch with body curled, ringed tail wrapped around the branch for balance, face turned to show the white mask
animals.rest_pose_for_species: curled on a bamboo branch with ringed tail tucked around body, head resting on forepaws
animals.anatomy_must_include: RUSSET-RED walnut-brown body fill; RINGED BUSHY TAIL with ALTERNATING russet-and-cream bands (mandatory — the rings are THE diagnostic, never a plain tail); WHITE FACE MASK with russet TEAR-LINE markings from eye down cheek; BLACK LEGS (mandatory contrast); rounded ears with white interior; the result must read as RED PANDA, never confused with a cat, fox, or raccoon.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: small vermillion tikka between the white-masked eyes
EAR INTERIOR: folk-cream panel with small leaf-vein motifs
FACE MASK: the white face mask preserved as a cream folk panel with russet tear-line accents
BODY: russet flank rosettes in vermillion + saffron with leaf-vein border motifs
RINGED TAIL: the alternating russet-and-cream bands rendered as folk-color rings — each band decorated with simple dot accents, the rings themselves being the primary ornament
BLACK LEGS: the four black legs rendered with vermillion + saffron anklets at each ankle

anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 1 in side profile (in white face mask)
anatomical_count_constraints.tail: 1 LONG BUSHY with ALTERNATING RUSSET-AND-CREAM RINGS (mandatory)
anatomical_count_constraints.tear_lines: russet markings from eyes down cheeks (mandatory)

animals.conservation_note: Sikkim's state animal. Khangchendzonga National Park hosts one of the densest red panda populations in India, sharing habitat with the Himalayan black bear and clouded leopard. Population fewer than 10,000 globally.

animals.signature_features: stocky goat-relative with HEAVY SHAGGY MANE around shoulders and chest (males have full mane down to forelegs), short backward-curving horns (both sexes, smaller in females), reddish-brown coat in summer becoming darker shaggy chestnut in winter, robust short legs adapted to cliff-faces. The DENSE SHOULDER MANE is the visual signature — the silhouette should read as MANED and SHAGGY, not smooth like a deer.
animals.signature_action: climbing a steep cliff face with all four hooves planted on small ledges, head turned uphill, mane fluffed by wind
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing on a precipitous ledge with all four legs planted, head lowered slightly to graze, mane settled around shoulders
animals.anatomy_must_include: HEAVY SHAGGY MANE around shoulders and chest in males (mandatory — without it the silhouette reads as a generic goat); short backward-curving horns; walnut-brown / russet body fill (NOT plain brown like a deer); short stocky legs (NOT long like a deer); cloven hooves; the result must read as HIMALAYAN TAHR (cliff-dwelling maned wild goat-relative), not bharal, not goral, not domestic goat.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: vermillion tikka between the horns
HORNS: gold-leaf accent along the ridge with folk-band rings at base
MANE (most important): the shaggy shoulder mane rendered as a dense folk-line pattern flowing from the neck down to the forelegs, in vermillion + saffron + leaf-green parallel lines
BODY: ornate flank rosettes in vermillion + saffron, the shaggy texture continued as folk-line pattern down the body
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron folk bands
TAIL: short with folk-cream tip
animals.conservation_note: Native to the southern Himalayan slopes from Khangchendzonga westward; declined due to habitat fragmentation and hunting. Khangchendzonga National Park's mid-elevation forests harbor a strong population.

animals.signature_features: male is RICH CRIMSON BODY covered with WHITE SPOTS each finely ringed with BLACK and a soft GRAY HALO (denser and more 'jewel-like' than the western tragopan); BLUE FACIAL SKIN around the eye and on cheeks; BLUE GULAR WATTLE at throat with TURQUOISE-AND-RED PATTERN visible when inflated; two SMALL BLUE HORN-LIKE FLESHY PROTRUSIONS on the crown during display (giving the genus its Greek 'horned-faun' name). Eastern Himalayan endemic, distinct from the western tragopan.
animals.signature_action: full courtship display: gular wattle inflated showing turquoise-and-red pattern, fleshy 'horns' erect on crown, body posture forward with white-spotted breast prominent
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched on a low branch with body horizontal, head turned to display the blue face and gular markings
animals.anatomy_must_include: CRIMSON body with WHITE-RINGED-IN-BLACK SPOTS (mandatory ringed-spot pattern); BLUE FACIAL SKIN; INFLATED BLUE GULAR WATTLE with turquoise-and-red interior pattern during display; FLESHY CROWN 'HORNS' visible in display poses; both feet visible; the result must read as SATYR TRAGOPAN specifically (the eastern Himalayan species with denser spotting and the wattle pattern), distinct from western tragopan.
required_decoration_zones: CROWN: black with two small fleshy 'horns' visible in display, accented with vermillion + saffron
FACE: blue facial skin preserved as a clean folk panel with vermillion eye ring
GULAR WATTLE: blue lappet with TURQUOISE-AND-RED FOLK PATTERN — the wattle pattern is itself a Madhubani-vocabulary ornament with concentric folk forms
BREAST + BODY: crimson body with WHITE-IN-BLACK-RING dots scattered rhythmically — the ringed-spot pattern is itself folk-ornament-friendly
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + crimson
TAIL: short black with folk-bar tip
FEET: both visible with vermillion ankle bands

anatomical_count_constraints.fleshy_horns: 2 small fleshy crown protrusions (in display pose)

animals.conservation_note: Eastern Himalayan endemic — Sikkim's most beautiful pheasant. Khangchendzonga's mid-altitude rhododendron forests are critical habitat; population threatened by habitat loss and trapping for the cage-bird trade.

animals.signature_features: stocky high-altitude partridge with BLACK-AND-WHITE FINELY BARRED plumage across most of the body (a delicate barring pattern, NOT large blotches), a distinctive CHESTNUT BREAST BAND below the throat, red-pink legs and bill, compact rounded silhouette adapted to high-altitude wind. The fine-barred BLACK-AND-WHITE pattern + chestnut breast band is the unmistakable combination.
animals.signature_action: running across rocky alpine ground with body lowered and head forward, wings tucked tight
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert on a snowy rock with both feet planted, head turned, body compact
animals.anatomy_must_include: FINELY BLACK-AND-WHITE BARRED body plumage (NOT large blotches, NOT solid color — the FINE BARRING is the diagnostic); distinctive CHESTNUT BREAST BAND below the throat (mandatory diagnostic); RED-PINK legs and bill (specific to this species); compact rounded body; the result must read as SNOW PARTRIDGE specifically, not a chukar or generic partridge.
required_decoration_zones: CROWN + HEAD: black-and-white barred pattern translated as folk-stripe ornament
THROAT: cream folk panel with chestnut breast-band rendered as a vermillion + saffron folk-band
BREAST + BODY: the finely barred plumage rendered as a sequence of parallel folk-stripes in indigo + cream, with rhythmic dot accents
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + leaf-green
TAIL: short barred with folk-pattern tip
FEET: red-pink folk-color rendered as vermillion ankle bands
animals.conservation_note: Specialist of the highest alpine zone (3500-5500 m) of the Himalaya; Khangchendzonga's alpine meadows above the treeline are core habitat. Range-restricted to the Himalayan and trans-Himalayan zone.

animals.signature_features: world's largest living reptile — MASSIVE ARMORED BODY with heavy dorsal scutes in distinct rows (the armored plates are themselves folk-pattern-ready), PALE YELLOWISH-TAN UNDERBELLY contrasting with the dark green-teal back, broad muscular tail nearly as long as the body, long crocodilian snout with visible interlocking teeth, prominent ridged brow over the yellow vertical-slit eye. Bulkier and longer-snouted than mugger crocodile.
animals.signature_action: lunging from water with body half-emerged, tail whipping the surface into scattering folk-dots, jaw open to display teeth
animals.rest_pose_for_species: basking on a mud bank with body fully extended, head turned slightly, tail laid flat behind, mouth slightly open in thermoregulation
animals.anatomy_must_include: ARMORED DORSAL SCUTES in distinct rows along back and tail (mandatory diagnostic — never a smooth-skinned crocodilian); PALE YELLOWISH-TAN BELLY contrasting with dark forest-teal back; LONG broad-based snout (NOT the shorter mugger snout, NOT the narrow gharial snout); 4 legs all visible; long broad tail nearly body-length; yellow vertical-slit eye visible under ridged brow; the result must read as SALTWATER CROCODILE (largest, longest-snouted, most-armored) not mugger and not gharial.
required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD + BROW: ridged brow rendered as folk-line pattern with vermillion eye ring around the yellow vertical-slit eye

DORSAL SCUTES (most important): each armor plate along the back rendered as an individual folk-pattern panel — alternating leaf-vein motifs, rosettes, dot-clusters in vermillion + saffron + leaf-green
NECK FOLDS: armored neck collar in indigo + leaf-green
FOUR LEGS: leg armor rendered as folk scale-pattern, with vermillion ankle bands at each foot
TAIL SCUTES: continuation of dorsal scute pattern as folk-rings along the tail
BELLY: pale yellowish-tan underbelly preserved as a folk-cream panel with vermillion border at the body-belly seam
JAW: long jaw with folk-pattern teeth visible as small ornament-rhythm marks

anatomical_count_constraints.dorsal_scute_rows: multiple rows from neck to tail tip (mandatory armor pattern)

animals.conservation_note: Bhitarkanika hosts India's largest saltwater crocodile population (~1700 individuals, including individuals over 6m long). The world's largest verified saltwater crocodile in captivity (over 7m) came from this delta. Project Crocodile (1975) launched its conservation here.

animals.signature_features: reddish-brown coat (which the Madhubani body fill anchors to deep-indigo) covered with PROMINENT WHITE SPOTS arranged in rows along the body (the spots are the unmistakable diagnostic — Axis axis = the spotted axis), white belly, branched antlers in males (three tines: brow + main + back), slender athletic build with white throat patch. Smaller and more delicate than sambar.
animals.signature_action: leaping mid-stride with all four feet airborne, antlers held forward, body extended in classic bounding leap
animals.rest_pose_for_species: standing alert with head turned and ears forward, all four feet planted, antlers visible in profile
animals.anatomy_must_include: WHITE SPOTS arranged in HORIZONTAL ROWS along the body (mandatory — never random scatter, never absent — these are THE defining feature); WHITE BELLY clearly demarcated; branched antlers in males (3 tines); white throat patch; slender body NOT bulky like sambar; the result must read as CHITAL (the spotted deer of India) and never confused with hog deer, barasingha, or sambar.
