required_decoration_zones: FOREHEAD: vermillion tikka between the antlers
ANTLERS: gold-leaf accent along the tines with folk-band rings at base
NECK: folk collar in indigo + leaf-green with white throat patch rendered as a folk-cream panel

BODY: the WHITE SPOTS preserved as a rhythmic ornament — each spot a small white circle with vermillion or saffron folk-dot center, arranged in horizontal rows along the back and flanks; the spots are themselves the primary ornament
WHITE BELLY: preserved as a cream folk panel with vermillion border at the body-belly seam
FOUR LEG ANKLETS: vermillion + saffron folk bands
TAIL: short with white folk-cream underside visible in motion

anatomical_count_constraints.antlers: 2 branched with 3 tines each (males) or no antlers (females)

animals.conservation_note: Bhitarkanika's mangrove edges and grassland margins host a healthy chital population, which is the primary prey base for the park's tigers (occasionally visiting from Similipal) and the resident saltwater crocodiles at the waterline.

animals.signature_features: large stork (1.1m tall) with NEARLY BALD YELLOWISH-PINK HEAD AND NECK (sparse downy feathers, almost vulture-like), MASSIVE YELLOW DAGGER BILL, dark slate-gray-to-black back and wings, WHITE BELLY and white underwing, long pink-orange legs. The bald yellowish head + huge dagger bill + dark-and-white body is the unmistakable adjutant silhouette.
animals.signature_action: standing tall with neck stretched up and bill pointed skyward, wings half-spread, drying in the sun after a swamp feeding
animals.rest_pose_for_species: hunched on a tree branch with neck retracted into shoulders, bill tucked downward, long legs visible below
animals.anatomy_must_include: NEARLY BALD YELLOWISH-PINK HEAD AND NECK (mandatory diagnostic — NOT feathered like a heron, NOT crested like a sarus crane); MASSIVE YELLOW DAGGER BILL (proportional to head, must look heavy); DARK back and wings WITH WHITE BELLY (the contrast is diagnostic); LONG PINK-ORANGE LEGS; both feet visible; the result must read as LESSER ADJUTANT (a stork, not a vulture, not a crane).
required_decoration_zones: HEAD + NECK: the bald yellowish-pink skin rendered as a folk-cream panel with sparse small folk-dot 'down' marks; small vermillion accent on the bill base
BILL: massive yellow dagger rendered with gold-leaf folk accent
BREAST + BACK (dark zone): the dark back rendered as deep-indigo folk panels with leaf-vein motifs in subtler tones, anchored by the body's primary indigo fill
WHITE BELLY: preserved as a cream folk panel with vermillion border at the body-belly seam
WINGS: folk feather bars in indigo + cream
LEGS: long pink-orange legs rendered as folk-vermillion panels with cream ankle bands
FEET: both visible with vermillion ankle bands

anatomical_count_constraints.eyes_visible: 1 in side profile (set in bald skin)
anatomical_count_constraints.head_feathering: nearly bald (mandatory — NOT a feathered head)

animals.conservation_note: Bhitarkanika's mangrove and adjacent wetland complex is one of the most important breeding sites for the lesser adjutant in eastern India. Population threatened by wetland loss and human disturbance at nesting trees.

animals.signature_features: large coastal raptor with PURE WHITE HEAD, BREAST, BELLY, AND UNDERWING contrasting with SLATE-GRAY back and upper wings, SHORT WHITE TAIL with a black band at the base (visible only in flight), heavy hooked yellow-and-gray bill, yellow legs with massive black talons. The clear WHITE-AND-GRAY two-tone is the unmistakable adult diagnostic.
animals.signature_action: diving toward water with wings half-folded, talons extended forward, body angled steeply, prey targeted below
animals.rest_pose_for_species: perched on a high mangrove branch with wings folded, white head turned to scan, body upright and alert
animals.anatomy_must_include: PURE WHITE HEAD, BREAST, BELLY (mandatory — not pale gray, not buff); SLATE-GRAY BACK AND UPPER WINGS (mandatory contrast); HEAVY HOOKED YELLOW-AND-GRAY BILL; YELLOW LEGS with MASSIVE BLACK TALONS; the white-and-gray two-tone must be SHARP not gradient; the result must read as ADULT WHITE-BELLIED SEA EAGLE (not a juvenile, not a fish eagle, not a brahminy kite).
required_decoration_zones: HEAD (white zone): the pure white head preserved as a cream folk panel with vermillion eye ring and small folk-dot at the bill base
BILL: heavy hooked yellow-and-gray bill with gold-leaf folk accent along the cere
BREAST + BELLY (white zone): preserved as a cream folk panel with minimal vermillion + saffron rosette accents at the chest center — do NOT overdecorate the white
BACK + UPPER WINGS (gray zone): slate-gray rendered as a deeper indigo folk panel with leaf-vein motifs and folk feather bars
TAIL: short white with a black band at base, rendered as a folk-bar pattern
LEGS + TALONS: yellow legs with vermillion + saffron ankle bands; massive black talons rendered with bold folk-black outline
EYE: vermillion eye ring around the dark yellow iris

anatomical_count_constraints.tail: 1 short white with black base band
anatomical_count_constraints.talons: massive black hooked claws visible on visible foot

animals.conservation_note: Apex coastal raptor of Bhitarkanika's mangrove-estuarine ecosystem; pair-bonds for life and returns to the same tall nest tree for decades. Indicator species for healthy mangrove tidal systems.

_collaborator_notes: v2.0.0 catalog: 40 animals across 20 Indian national parks, two flagship species per park, organized as a single 'indian' series. This is the deliberate foundation for a Madhubani T-shirt collection and an eventual LoRA fine-tune. Every entry MUST include: slug, display_name, binomial, series, park, park_state, ordinal_in_series, body_type, body_fill_color, body_fill_color_name, signature_features, signature_action, rest_pose_for_species, iucn_status, conservation_note, seed_block_start. The optional anatomy_must_include field SHOULD be added for any species with strong pretrained signals likely to override our folk-art register (big cats whose natural orange/tawny coloring competes with the walnut anchor; species with diagnostic field marks like the rhino's single horn, the sloth bear's chest blaze, the red panda's ringed tail, the Irrawaddy dolphin's beakless head, the skimmer's elongated lower mandible). seed_block_start values are unique 100-step increments within the indigo seed block (8200-12200); see the per-entry values for actual allocation. Body fill follows the v2 convention: deep-indigo #1a2952 as default; walnut-brown #5a3a1f for big cats and sloth bear AND red panda AND indian fox (warm-fur species); forest-teal #1f4a3f for forest snakes and forest hornbills. Naturally colorful species (peacock, sarus crane, painted stork, greater flamingo, golden langur, nilgiri langur, red panda, indian giant squirrel) keep their species-specific colors on inner folk panels over a fundamental anchor body fill — they do NOT become uniformly indigo. The african and bird series from v1.0.0 are deprecated and preserved only as historical record in _deprecated_series.
